content:
The bell above the door chimed, a thin, silver sound that barely cut through the heavy humidity of the afternoon. I didn't look up immediately. My hands—my mother’s hands, broad at the knuckles and steady as stone—were busy. I was polishing the glass of the display case, watching my own reflection ripple over the velvet trays of obsidian and dried hyssop.

Eleven years. It’s a long time to stand i
